metadata.notes: agent loads AGENTS.md into context + records assistant text verbatim, and the pi adapter now scans full text blocks for the task-estimate marker (port landed 1ae457d9); the sole remaining blocker is the driver, which has no instruction-file-seeding primitive

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full (per-adapter marker scan landed in the pi parser — 1ae457d9)
driver_capability: gap:seed_instruction

route: driver-gap

## Reasoning

The scenario is agent-agnostic: any agent that (a) loads a persistent instruction file into context and (b) records its assistant prose verbatim in a local transcript can satisfy it. Pi clears both bars. The daemon pillar was a `bug` only because the pi adapter had no per-adapter marker scan; that port landed (commit 1ae457d9), so the daemon is now `full` and the sole remaining blocker is tooling — a missing workspace-seed driver step.

### agent = yes

Pi loads a persistent instruction file into the model's context. `pi`'s `loadProjectContextFiles` (src/core/resource-loader.ts, shipped in dist source maps) reads `["AGENTS.md", "CLAUDE.md"]` from the agent dir (`~/.pi/agent`) AND walks the cwd ancestor chain, loading each match. The loaded files flow through `getAgentsFiles()` into `_baseSystemPromptOptions.contextFiles` (src/core/agent-session.ts ~L912), so the marker rule reaches the model the same way the scenario assumes. Interactive mode even lists them under a "Context" section. Pi records assistant text verbatim: a committed pi recording stores assistant prose as the literal `message.content[].text` string (e.g. "ok", "alive") — an HTML comment in pi's prose would survive on disk byte-for-byte. So the agent *can* emit the marker and the transcript *would* retain it.

The verdict is `yes`, not higher-confidence, because emission-compliance is unverified live: the 2026-05-31 marker experiment measured 0% exact-format compliance even on claudecode (the model rewrites the marker), and no live pi recording exists to prove pi actually emits a parseable marker when instructed. AGENTS.md preference order (AGENTS.md wins over CLAUDE.md, and the global `~/.pi/agent/AGENTS.md` is loaded first) is the seam to seed against. Whether pi strips bare HTML comments from the instruction file at injection (the documented Claude Code hazard that forced the fenced-code-block workaround) is also unverified for pi — the scenario process already mandates fencing the example, which neutralizes this regardless.

### daemon = full

The task-estimate plumbing is fully shared and agent-agnostic, and the last adapter-specific piece — the per-adapter content scan — now exists for pi. Commit 1ae457d9 promoted the scan to `tailer.ScanTaskEstimate` (core/pkg/tailer/taskestimate_scan.go, shared like ExtractAssistantText) and wired it into the pi parser: pi/parser.go:88-107 walks `message.content[]` on assistant messages and, for each `text` block, calls `ScanTaskEstimate(text, ev.Timestamp)`, setting `ev.TaskEstimate` (parser.go:100-101). Critically the scan runs over the FULL block text, NOT the tail-truncated `extractPiAssistantText` display string, so a marker early in a long message survives (unit test TestParser_TaskEstimate_SurvivesLongMessage). User-role messages are ignored (TestParser_TaskEstimate_UserMessageIgnored). From there the rest of the pipeline was already adapter-independent and ready: the tailer (core/pkg/tailer/tailer.go lastTaskEstimate; tailer_metrics.go:320 surfaces it on every pass like the rate-limit snapshot), the domain projection (core/domain/session/task_estimate.go ForecastTaskCompletion: eta = now + (total-completed)*(elapsed/completed)), and the metrics adapter (core/adapters/outbound/metrics/adapter.go:176-181 populates TaskEstimate + TaskCompletionEta). So given a recording in which pi emits a parseable marker, irrlichd observes task_estimate.{total_rounds,completed_rounds} and burns down task_completion_eta exactly as the scenario asserts. The trace exists on disk (verbatim assistant text) AND the daemon now handles it → `daemon=full`. (This is provisional on live emission: no pi recording exists yet, so the scan is proven by unit tests but not by a websocket-vector observation — confidence stays 0.8 and `record` settles it.)

### driver = gap:seed_instruction (UNCHANGED)

The recipe's first step (scenario process step 1) must install the marker rule into a persistent instruction file before pi launches — either the scratch workspace's `<cwd>/AGENTS.md` or the global `~/.pi/agent/AGENTS.md`. The pi interactive driver cannot do this. Its step grammar (DRIVE_ELICITS, driver-interactive.sh:161) is `send slash wait_turn interrupt keys sleep exit_clean resume reset_session start_session session` — there is no `seed_instruction`/`write_file` step, and `step_start_session` just `mkdir -p $RUN_CWD` + launches pi with no file seeding. No driver in the repo has an instruction-file-seeding primitive (verified: codex/aider/opencode drivers don't seed AGENTS.md, and run-cell.sh does NOT execute the recipe's `setup` array — it is documentation-only, so a `setup`-described seed never actually runs). This matches the opencode cell's verdict (`gap:seed_instruction`) for the same scenario. (In production the rule is installed out-of-band by the daemon's managed-block installer — claudecode patches `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md`; the pi parallel is `~/.pi/agent/AGENTS.md`. But a self-contained recording must seed it as a recipe step, which the driver lacks.) This is tooling work, not an observability limit: `record` ports a `seed_instruction` step (write the fenced marker rule into `$RUN_CWD/AGENTS.md` before launch) into the pi driver, then drives.

## What a clean recording asserts (once the driver gap closes)

The spec asserts the user-observable arc + the task_estimate metric vector while working: task_estimate.{total_rounds,completed_rounds} present and matching the agent's report, completed_rounds non-decreasing reaching total_rounds, updated_at advancing, and task_completion_eta burning down (later eta <= earlier eta). Suppression-at-ready ("fields absent at ready") is enforced at the PRESENTATION layer — `taskEtaPresentation` returns null when state != working (platforms/web/irrlicht.test.js:366-369) — not by clearing the raw websocket metric, so that criterion is a UI assertion, not a websocket-vector assertion. The spec asserts the lifecycle arc + the metric presence/burndown while working; suppression-at-ready is left to the chip layer.
caveats: Live emission unverified: no pi recording exists, and the 2026-05-31 experiment showed 0% exact-format marker compliance even on claudecode (the model rewrites the marker). agent=yes is doc/code-grounded (pi loads AGENTS.md, records verbatim text) and daemon=full is code-grounded (the scan landed + unit-tested), but record must promote both from provisional to settled with a live websocket observation.

Whether pi strips bare HTML comments from AGENTS.md at context-injection (the claudecode hazard) is unverified for pi; the scenario process already mandates fencing the marker example, which neutralizes it.

daemon=full as of 1ae457d9: the pi parser scans the FULL message.content[] text blocks via the shared tailer.ScanTaskEstimate (NOT the 200-rune-truncated extractPiAssistantText display string), so markers early in long messages survive. Proven by unit tests (parse / truncation-trap / user-ignored); not yet by a live recording.
Suppression-at-ready is a UI-layer concern (taskEtaPresentation), so the spec asserts the working-state metric arc + burndown, not the absence of the raw field on the ready-state websocket frame.
route stays driver-gap: record must port a seed_instruction step into the pi driver (no reference driver has it — same gap as the opencode cell) before it can drive this cell.

sources.note: pi/parser.go:88-107 walks message.content[] on assistant messages and feeds each text block's FULL text to tailer.ScanTaskEstimate, setting ev.TaskEstimate (L100-101). The marker scan landed in commit 1ae457d9; it runs over the full block, not the tail-truncated extractPiAssistantText display string.

The shared, adapter-agnostic marker scan (tailer.ScanTaskEstimate) promoted from claudecode's scanTaskEstimate in 1ae457d9 — like ExtractAssistantText. Tolerant parse, latest-valid-marker wins, caps payload at 2048 bytes; each adapter walks its own transcript shape and feeds full text blocks here.

TestParser_TaskEstimate_FromAssistantText (parse 3/8), TestParser_TaskEstimate_SurvivesLongMessage (marker early in a 200+rune message survives the display truncation), TestParser_TaskEstimate_UserMessageIgnored — added in 1ae457d9; all pass (go test ./core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/... -run TaskEstimate).
L320 surfaces lastTaskEstimate on every metrics pass (agent-agnostic, like rate-limit). Shared plumbing was already ready; with the pi scan landed the full pipeline is wired for pi.

loadProjectContextFiles reads [AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md] from the agent dir + walks the cwd ancestor chain; feeds getAgentsFiles() → contextFiles. Authoritative evidence pi loads a persistent instruction file. pi 0.70.0 (@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent).
loadedContextFiles = resourceLoader.getAgentsFiles().agentsFiles passed as contextFiles into _baseSystemPromptOptions — the AGENTS.md content reaches the model's system prompt.
pi records assistant prose verbatim as literal message.content[].text ("ok", "alive") — an HTML comment marker would survive on disk.

DRIVE_ELICITS (L161): send slash wait_turn interrupt keys sleep exit_clean resume reset_session start_session session — no seed_instruction/write_file step. step_start_session just mkdir+launch, no AGENTS.md seeding. This is the remaining blocker (driver=gap:seed_instruction).

Sibling cell with the SAME driver gap (gap:seed_instruction) for this scenario: 'No driver in the repo has any instruction-file-seeding primitive.' run-cell.sh does not execute the recipe `setup` array, so the codex/aider driver=ready (which relied on `setup` seeding) is the optimistic call; pi follows opencode's honest gap verdict.

In production the rule is installed out-of-band into ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md (managed block, fenced example). L37 names the per-agent parallels incl. AGENTS.md dirs; pi's parallel is ~/.pi/agent/AGENTS.md. A self-contained recording must seed it via a recipe step instead.
taskEtaPresentation returns null when state != working — suppression-at-ready is a UI-layer concern, not a raw-websocket-vector assertion.

preconditions: pi CLI on PATH and authenticated (pi --version → 0.70.0)
irrlichd is recording (dev daemon with --record; prod daemon has no --record)
setup: BLOCKED on driver gap: the pi interactive driver needs a seed_instruction step that writes the fenced marker rule into <cwd>/AGENTS.md (or ~/.pi/agent/AGENTS.md) BEFORE launching pi. record ports this step (no reference driver has it — same gap as the opencode cell), then drives.
The seeded AGENTS.md MUST wrap the marker example in a fenced code block (some agents strip bare HTML comments at injection).

script.text:
## Task progress markers

As you work a multi-step task, emit a hidden progress marker on each turn so tools can show a completion estimate. Emit it as an HTML comment in your reply:

```
<!-- {"marker":"irrlicht-eta","total_rounds":N,"completed_rounds":M} -->
```

`total_rounds` is your estimate of the task's phases; `completed_rounds` is how many you have finished. Update `completed_rounds` as you progress.

Plan a 4-phase task: create dirs a/, b/, c/, then write README.md summarizing them. Before doing anything, reply with the progress marker at 0/4. Then do ONE phase per turn, emitting an updated marker each turn, until 4/4.
Do the next phase and update the marker.
Do the next phase and update the marker.
Do the final phase, write README.md, and emit the marker at 4/4.

verify: While working: task_estimate.{total_rounds=4, completed_rounds matching the agent's report} present on session metrics.
completed_rounds non-decreasing across turns, reaching 4 by the final working turn; updated_at advances per fresher marker.
task_completion_eta present once >=1 round reported, burning down (later eta <= earlier eta).
State arc ready -> working -> ready; final state ready.
